methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Arbe Robotics is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $118M to date. The valuation universe for this company is constrained: while six private peers operate in Drones & Autonomous—including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and Aurora Innovation—there are no public comparables in this narrow sector. This composition limits the breadth of comparable company analysis.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains only four same-stage peers, but filtering by stage, adjacent maturity tiers, and valuation proximity yields fewer than three datapoints in any single filter combination. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial category risk, as Drones & Autonomous comprises heterogeneous business models (passenger aircraft, delivery systems, perception software) with structurally different unit economics and paths to profitability. Without sufficient narrow-band comparables or public market benchmarks, traditional peer-multiple triangulation cannot be reliably executed.

Valuation of Arbe therefore depends on alternative methods not detailed in the provided dataset—such as DCF analysis, venture capital method, or revenue multiple approaches applied to private peer data. The primary uncertainty is the absence of public market anchors and the thinness of the private peer set at Arbe's specific stage. Any valuation conclusion requires explicit reliance on non-comparable-company inputs and clear articulation of stage-specific risk adjustments.
